ranked.problem: AUTHORITATIVE compose path _compose_section_per_basket (plain def, verified_compose.py:3262) is called with no await/to_thread from async _run_section — the entire per-basket compose+verify+redraft tail (85-88% of section wall clock) runs ON the event loop, freezing all sibling sections, all writer HTTP callbacks, and every judge time.sleep backoff (entailment_judge.py:1166 15-60s 429 sleeps land on the loop). PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=2 is fiction; achieved compose concurrency is exactly 1. This is the identical bug class 0615bc5 fixed in abstractive_writer.py, one layer up, still at HEAD. (Dedups findings at :5468, :5514, verified_compose.py:3325 caller-side, and the time.sleep-on-loop finding.)
ranked.fix: At BOTH call sites wrap: `_vc_composed = await asyncio.to_thread(functools.partial(_compose_section_per_basket, _vc_baskets, evidence_pool, writer_fn=_vc_writer_fn, verify_fn=_vc_verify_fn, edges=getattr(credibility_analysis, 'edges', None), redraft_fn=_vc_redraft_fn, ...))` — identical remedy to 0615bc5. Note _redraft's inner asyncio.run (:4994) stays correct off-loop. Commit this together with the three env wins BEFORE any refactor. Never touch verify_fn semantics (faithfulness gate unchanged).
ranked.acceptance: During phase-2 of a section: (a) event-loop heartbeat probe (asyncio task logging monotonic gaps every 1s) shows max gap < 2s where it previously froze for minutes; (b) log shows two sections' phase-2 progress lines interleaved (achieved section concurrency >1); (c) a 429 backoff sleep no longer stalls sibling sections' writer POSTs.

ranked.problem: Every NLI judge POST is gated by a process-global threading.BoundedSemaphore sized by PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY — VERIFIED read at judge_concurrency.py:49/81-99, default DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY=4 at :55, acquired around every POST at entailment_judge.py:155 — and VERIFIED set in NO compose launcher and no run_s5_i3.py setdefault. Once fix #1 lands, all verify threads queue behind 4 slots; the 0615bc5 pre-pass speedup is itself capped at 4. The cap was tuned for a credibility-burst 429 storm, not compose; 0 429s at 128-way proves rate limit is not the ceiling. (Dedups 5 duplicate findings on judge_concurrency.py:55/:178.)
ranked.fix: Add `export PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY=16` to launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh AND `os.environ.setdefault("PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY", "16")` in the run_s5_i3.py:342-359 setdefault block (launcher/env still wins). Pure config, zero code change. Optional follow-up: phase-aware override via the existing _CONCURRENCY_OVERRIDE mechanism (judge_concurrency.py:61) so the de-storm stays tight for the credibility burst.
ranked.acceptance: Startup log / one-line probe shows resolve_max_concurrency() == 16 (knob actually read); during compose, >4 judge POSTs observed concurrently in flight (in-flight counter or timestamp-overlap on judge POST start/end logs); 429 rate stays ~0.

ranked.problem: strict_verify's per-sentence entailment loop runs SERIAL in production: _parallel_verify_workers() returns 1 when PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY is unset (VERIFIED read at provenance_generator.py:3390; serial gate `if _verify_workers < 2` at :3664), and the knob is set in no compose launcher and no run_s5_i3.py setdefault. The bounded ThreadPoolExecutor path (:3678-3718, contextvars-copied, cost-reconciled, already gated at 16 in scripts/iarch011_parallel_verify_gate.py) is dead code in production. strict_verify runs twice per section (:5667, :5804).
ranked.fix: Add `os.environ.setdefault("PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY", "8")` to the run_s5_i3.py:342-359 block and `export PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY=8` to launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh. Zero code change; the parallel path preserves original-order reassembly so kept/dropped verdicts are byte-identical to serial.
ranked.acceptance: Log/probe shows _parallel_verify_workers() == 8 and strict_verify enters the ThreadPoolExecutor branch (knob actually read); strict_verify wall time for a multi-sentence section drops several-fold; kept/dropped verdict set identical to a serial control on the same fixture.

ranked.problem: A hung judge POST holds a semaphore slot for the full PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S=300s (launcher raised it from the 150s code default without raising the 4-slot pool), and PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES — VERIFIED read via _ENV_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES at entailment_judge.py:199, whose own comment at :196 says the run slate is supposed to set it to 1 — is set NOWHERE, defaulting to 2, so one sentence can burn 3x300s = 900s of slot time. (Dedups both slot-hold findings.)
ranked.fix: Add `export PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES=1` to launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh + setdefault in run_s5_i3.py (the value the code comment prescribes). Separately consider lowering PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S from 300 back toward 150 (measured p90 13-14s) as a follow-up measured against blank-200 recurrence, since Fix-6 raised it deliberately. Do not weaken fail-closed behavior.
ranked.acceptance: Probe shows resolved total-deadline retries == 1 (knob actually read); a forced-hang test shows a single sentence holds a judge slot for at most 2 x total_s before fail-closed drop, not 3x.

ranked.problem: get_semaphore() rebinds the module-global asyncio.Semaphore whenever the running loop differs (VERIFIED: `if _LLM_SEMAPHORE is None or _LLM_SEMAPHORE_LOOP is not running_loop` rebuilds at full cap), and _redraft creates a fresh ThreadPoolExecutor + `asyncio.run(_run())` per re-draft — a brand-new loop each time — so main-loop/redraft-loop alternation destroys the semaphore ~300x/run, in-flight permits vanish, and PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM bounds nothing (unbounded bursts feed the 429/connection-error storms). Note the judge transport bypasses this semaphore entirely (entailment_judge builds its own httpx client), so the judge cap (fix #2) is the only judge bound. (Dedups 2 findings.)
ranked.fix: In llm_provider.py: key semaphores per-loop in a WeakKeyDictionary[loop -> Semaphore] instead of clobbering one global. In multi_section_generator._redraft: reuse one persistent worker loop (or make _redraft async and await _call_writer on the caller's loop) instead of asyncio.run per call. Keep the blocking fut.result off the event loop (guaranteed once fix #1 lands).
ranked.acceptance: 'Concurrency semaphore (re)initialized' log line appears once per distinct loop (<=2-3 per run), not 300+; instrumented in-flight OpenRouterClient call count never exceeds PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM across a full run with redrafts.

ranked.problem: 94% of baskets (304-326 of 346) land in ONE residual section coroutine that is scheduled LAST behind Semaphore(2) — it starts 50-90 min in and is a single indivisible serial unit, so the run makespan is one serial section and PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS has nothing to parallelize. (Dedups 2 findings; do AFTER the env+await commits.)
ranked.fix: Two independent changes in run_s5_i3.py: (1) shard the residual section's baskets into ceil(N/16)-basket sub-sections composed as separate tasks under sec_sema (basket rendering is independent; reassemble by index, dedup on the concatenated list) and raise PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS to ~4-6 to match; (2) create section tasks sorted by len(section.ev_ids) descending so the biggest work starts at t=0. Also checkpoint at basket/shard granularity so a kill does not discard 95 min of work.
ranked.acceptance: Log shows the residual section's shards STARTing at t~0 and >1 concurrent '[section N] START' with N>=4; total baskets processed across shards == 346 with no dupes/drops in the concatenated output; makespan no longer equals the residual section's solo time.

ranked.problem: Even off-loop (after fix #1), _compose_section_per_basket is a strictly serial basket-after-basket, sentence-after-sentence loop — one blocking ~8.9s judge POST at a time — so intra-section verify concurrency is 1 and the raised judge cap (fix #2) is only exploitable across sections/shards. (Dedups 3 fan-out findings.)
ranked.fix: Fan out over baskets with a bounded ThreadPoolExecutor using the battle-tested contextvars-copy pattern from provenance_generator.py:3678-3718 (_parent_ctx.copy().run), workers ~= PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY; verdicts are order-independent, reassemble by index. Optionally also parallelize the per-sentence verify inside _compose_one_basket the same way. Do NOT change verify_fn/redraft semantics — the faithfulness gate stays byte-identical per sentence.
ranked.acceptance: During one section's phase-2, >1 judge POST concurrently in flight (overlapping POST start/end timestamps); section compose output on a fixture is identical (same kept sentences, same [CITE]/[#calc] tokens) to the serial path; per-run judge telemetry (FX-09 ContextVar) still reconciles.

ranked.problem: async coherence_rewrite_section calls the SYNC verify_sentence_provenance per sentence in a bare for-loop — serial AND on the event loop (default-ON via PG_SECTION_COHERENCE_PASS), freezing all other sections for N_sentences x ~5-9s during every coherence pass.
ranked.fix: Hoist the whole re-verify sweep into one `await asyncio.to_thread(...)`; inside, use a bounded ThreadPoolExecutor over only the changed sentences (new != orig). Same acceptance discipline as fix #1; verify semantics untouched.
ranked.acceptance: Event-loop heartbeat gap stays < 2s during the coherence pass; only changed sentences trigger judge calls (log count matches diff count); re-verify verdicts identical to serial control.

ranked.problem: SHA-gated section RESUME never fires (0/5 attempts): a root daemon outside this worktree (/workspace/gear_sync.sh) overwrites the pinned cp3 path ~every 96 min while a 3h+ compose attempt runs, so every relaunch sees a SHA mismatch, declares all checkpoints stale, and recomposes from scratch — a livelock, not a slowdown. The daemon itself is outside the fence; fix must be entirely on our side.
ranked.fix: (a) In the launcher, COPY cp2/cp3/cp4 into the run's own output dir at attempt-1 start and pass the copies (immutable inputs for the attempt's lifetime; 'newest wins' applies between runs, not during one). (b) In run_s5_i3.py, namespace checkpoints per input-SHA (ckpt/<sha12>/section_N_draft.json) so a changed input opens a new namespace instead of discarding a valid one.
ranked.acceptance: Kill a run mid-wave and relaunch while gear_sync keeps mutating the shared cp3: relaunch logs resume-hit > 0 (loads at least the completed section_*_draft.json) and the inputs_sha check passes against the snapshot copies.

ranked.problem: One ThreadPoolExecutor + OS thread is created and torn down per judge POST, and the OpenRouter/httpx client is re-instantiated ~every 2.6 requests (1405 inits / 3693 POSTs), defeating connection pooling and TLS reuse — the likely proximate cause of the 562 'Connection error, retrying' warnings.
ranked.fix: Hoist one module-level ThreadPoolExecutor (sized >= judge cap) for the deadline-wall submits, and one long-lived judge httpx client per process, reused across POSTs. No verdict-logic change.
ranked.acceptance: 'OpenRouter client initialized' (and judge client build) appears a handful of times per run instead of ~1400; connection-error retry warnings drop by an order of magnitude on a comparable run.

ranked.problem: With 2 sections x PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY=12 = 24 writer tasks contending for the 16-slot global LLM semaphore, the per-stage writer knob is silently undercut whenever sections overlap — and after the sharding fix (#6) overlap becomes the norm.
ranked.fix: Raise PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM to >= sections x writer_conc (e.g. 32) in launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh, sequenced AFTER fix #5 so the cap is actually enforced, and watch 429 rate (0 429s at 128-way says there is headroom).
ranked.acceptance: Achieved writer concurrency (overlapping generate() call windows) reaches ~24 when two sections/shards overlap; 429 rate stays ~0.

ranked.problem: PG_S5_SPAN_CHAR_CAP=8000 is exported by all launchers but read by ZERO code lines (VERIFIED: grep over src/scripts/config = 0 hits) — spans enter the writer prompt uncapped (p90 11.4k chars, max 24.9k), and fat baskets (70-98s latency vs 4.3s for small) set the wave-makespan tail.
ranked.fix: Wire the knob at the MEMBER level, not by naive display truncation: trim via the existing _member_surviving_units_or_drop-style re-anchoring (dataclasses.replace + re-derived span) so the [#ev:eid:start-end] provenance token stays consistent with the shown text — never desync the region gate.
ranked.acceptance: grep shows a live reader of PG_S5_SPAN_CHAR_CAP; max writer prompt input tokens drops below ~4k on the real corpus; provenance region gate pass-rate unchanged on a fixture (no new token/span mismatches).

ranked.problem: Judge is one (span, sentence) pair per HTTP POST (~1k tokens of work paying a full 8.9-19.7s round trip) even where callers already hold the full sentence list — N-fold avoidable POST count and semaphore pressure.
ranked.fix: Add judge_batch(pairs) -> one prompt enumerating N SPAN/SENTENCE pairs returning a JSON array of N verdicts; reuse _extract_first_json_object fail-closed per element; on array-length mismatch fall back to per-pair calls (never pad — that fails OPEN); keep the verdict cache in front (batch only misses). Lowest priority: do only after fan-out fixes land and if judge POSTs remain the bottleneck.
ranked.acceptance: On a fixture, judge POST count drops ~N-fold with verdicts identical to per-pair calls; a deliberately malformed/short array triggers per-pair fallback (no verdict is ever silently padded).

result.sign_off_reason: Nothing is fixed yet — this round only verified the forensic findings against the compose_fix worktree and converted them into concrete fixes. Three P0s (sync-on-loop compose at multi_section_generator.py:5468/:5514, judge cap 4, serial strict_verify) and six P1s remain open.

sota.gap: Compose achieved concurrency is 1 (event loop pinned by the sync compose tail) with the NLI judge hard-capped at 4 and strict_verify serial, while 94% of baskets sit in one tail-scheduled section — versus the 16-24-way off-loop, sharded, judge-parallel pipeline the knobs already claim and 0-429s-at-128-way headroom proves the providers can absorb.
